chapter_goal: Show how atlases, tangent splitting, and integrability produce the Dolbeault complex used throughout Hodge theory.
checks: projectors onto type components are idempotent
toy bracket closure flag separates integrable and nonintegrable models
lab: Perturb a local almost complex matrix and inspect which algebraic identity fails first.
library_routes: Matplotlib for atlas and tangent splitting diagrams
NetworkX for dependency flow from atlas to Dolbeault complex
SymPy for a bracket closure toy check
pitfalls: assuming every almost complex structure is integrable
mixing real tangent rank with complex rank
forgetting that d splits only after complexification
proof_moves: compare local charts through transition functions
split tangent vectors into type (1,0) and type (0,1)
measure integrability by closure of a distribution under brackets
visual_focus: Overlapping charts and tangent cones show where the type decomposition lives and how bracket closure is tested.
